title: This is a draft for enabling opt-in tail-folding on vectorized epilogue.
author: hassnaaHamdi
description:
Enable tail-folding on vectorized epilogue, so that we can have:
Vectorized main loop
Tail-folded vectorized epilogue loop.

This is just a draft to show what we need to do.

tests.test_body:
target triple = "aarch64-linux-gnu"

define void @main_vf_vscale_x_16(ptr %A) #0 {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  store i8 1, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 1024
  br i1 %exitcond, label %for.body, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { "target-features"="+sve" }

target triple = "aarch64-linux-gnu"

define void @test_pr57912_pointer_induction(ptr %start) #0 {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %ptr.iv = phi ptr [ %start, %entry ], [ %ptr.iv.next, %loop ]
  store i8 0, ptr %ptr.iv, align 1
  %ptr.iv.next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr.iv, i64 1
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 10000
  br i1 %cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { "target-features"="+sve" }

target triple = "aarch64-linux-gnu"

; Function Attrs: vscale_range(8,8)
define void @main_vf_vscale_x_2(ptr %A, i64 %n) #0 {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  store i64 1, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exitcond, label %for.body, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { vscale_range(8,8) "target-features"="+sve" }

target triple = "aarch64-linux-gnu"

; Function Attrs: vscale_range(1,16)
define void @trip_count_vscale(ptr noalias %a, ptr noalias %b) #0 {
entry:
  %v = tail call i64 @llvm.vscale.i64()
  %n = mul nuw nsw i64 %v, 1033
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds nuw float, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %l.a = load float, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %arrayidx3 = getelementptr inbounds nuw float, ptr %b, i64 %iv
  %l.b = load float, ptr %arrayidx3, align 4
  %mul4 = fmul float %l.a, %l.b
  store float %mul4, ptr %arrayidx3, align 4
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %for.body

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(none)
declare i64 @llvm.vscale.i64() #1

attributes #0 = { vscale_range(1,16) "target-features"="+sve" }
attributes #1 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(none) }

target triple = "aarch64-linux-gnu"

; Function Attrs: vscale_range(8,8)
define void @main_vf_vscale_x_2_no_epi_iteration(ptr %A) #0 {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  store i64 1, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 1024
  br i1 %exitcond, label %for.body, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { vscale_range(8,8) "target-features"="+sve" }

target triple = "aarch64-linux-gnu"

; Function Attrs: vscale_range(1,16)
define void @trip_count_vscale_no_epilogue_iterations(ptr noalias %a, ptr noalias %b) #0 {
entry:
  %v = tail call i64 @llvm.vscale.i64()
  %n = mul nuw nsw i64 %v, 1024
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds nuw float, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %l.a = load float, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %arrayidx3 = getelementptr inbounds nuw float, ptr %b, i64 %iv
  %l.b = load float, ptr %arrayidx3, align 4
  %mul4 = fmul float %l.a, %l.b
  store float %mul4, ptr %arrayidx3, align 4
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %for.body

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body
  ret void
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(none)
declare i64 @llvm.vscale.i64() #1

attributes #0 = { vscale_range(1,16) "target-features"="+sve" }
attributes #1 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(none) }
